【Brent Nears $100 as Hormuz Disruption Persists, US-Iran Talks Uncertain】Brent crude rose nearly 5% to about $99.39 a barrel as restricted tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz kept supply concerns elevated, with only limited shipments moving amid a US naval blockade and Iranian threats. President Donald Trump said the US and Iran will “probably” meet over the weekend, though no date is set. The two-week ceasefire expires April 21, with no clear path to reopening the route or securing a broader agreement.

【Key Points of the Past 24 Hours - MKTNews】China Economy & Markets
- China home prices rise in tier-1 cities; declines ease elsewhere in March
- Hang Seng Tech Index rises 1.7% intraday, reclaims 5,000 level, hits highest since March 20
- China Q1 GDP +5.0% YoY, growth accelerates to 33.42 trn yuan
- China March survey-based jobless rate in 31 major cities at 5.3%, hits highest since Aug last year
- China stats bureau deputy head: daily token usage exceeds 140 trillion, up 40% from end of last year

【US Stocks Edge Higher as Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire Eases Tensions】US equities closed modestly higher, with the S&P 500 rising 0.26% above 7,000, the Nasdaq gaining 0.36% and the Dow up 0.24%, after Donald Trump said Israel and Lebanon agreed to a 10-day ceasefire. The move lifted sentiment amid ongoing US-Iran talks. Gains were supported by strong earnings from major companies and jobless claims falling to 207,000, though industrial production declined 0.5% in March, missing expectations.
